definitions.text: The process of gathering the ripened crop; harvesting.
examples: The constant rain made the harvest a nightmare this year.

definitions.text: The yield of harvesting, i.e., the gathered crops or fruits.
examples: This year's cotton harvest was great but the corn harvest was disastrous.

definitions.text: The product or result of any exertion or course of action; reward or consequences.
examples: The surveillance mission yielded a healthy harvest of intel.

definitions.text: To bring in a harvest; reap; glean.
examples: We harvested the apples in September already.

definitions.text: To take a living organism as part of a managed process to gather food or resources, often with the intention of maintaining a healthy population.
examples: An efficient rifle or shotgun can harvest a deer for venison.

definitions.text: To take (an organ) from an organ donor.
